body.title: [in_progress] harvest_mode flag: disable escalation, run DS at fixed tier to the wall
body.description:
**Problem:** the coding loop's objective is a completed build, and on a capability failure it ESCALATES to a stronger tier (2-hop walk). For the failure-harvesting testing phase that confounds the exact measurement we want: escalating mid-run mixes 'the cheap builder couldn't' with 'a stronger one could', so the builder starve-curve blurs and the stuck-signal is papered over. This weekend's architect paged files to budget-exhaustion with no 'I'm stuck' exit.

**Proposed shape:** a `harvest_mode` flag on the coding loop (AgenticLoop / ArchitectEditor / the DS driver). When ON: (a) the escalation walk is DISABLED — a capability failure does NOT re-dispatch at a higher difficulty; the run stays at a fixed tier and terminates at its turn/budget cap, surfacing the wall instead of climbing past it. (b) objective flips from 'reach DONE' to 'reach the wall and report it'. Hex is the flat-ish host, so burning tokens at a fixed tier is cost-safe. FLAG, not deletion — the escalation machinery is real and returns for production; harvest_mode is a testing-phase mode.

**Affected files:** the DS driver / domain policy that RE-DISPATCHES on LOOP_AVAILABILITY/capability-fail (the escalation WALK — NOTE: agentic_loop.run() explicitly has NO escalation walk, it runs ONE attempt and returns a LoopResult; the walk that increments escalation_hop lives in the DRIVER, not the loop — first sprint step is to LOCATE it), plus agentic_loop.py/architect_editor.py only to THREAD the flag. Do NOT touch device.dispatch's escalation ceiling — disable at the walk layer.

**⚠️ HIGH-inertia pre-approval (Akien, 2026-07-05):** the harvest-mode arrangement (escalation-off at fixed tier for the harvest phase) is APPROVED in design dialogue. This is an additive flag gating existing behavior, not a rewrite of the escalation machinery.

**Design rules:** flag defaults OFF (production unchanged); escalation code stays intact behind the flag; log the mode at loop entry; inference-proxy-only (route through the device, never a direct model call).

**Scope boundary:** the flag + escalation-disable ONLY. NOT the ladder/rung logic (next ticket), NOT drop-ticket, NOT resume.

**Validation:** a harvest_mode run on a capability-failing ticket logs 'harvest_mode: escalation disabled' and ends at the turn cap with escalation_hop still 0 (no higher-tier re-dispatch) — observable in the run log and the io_corpus records (no escalation_hop>0 entry for that ticket).

**Observable via:** log.info('AgenticLoop: harvest_mode=on — escalation disabled, fixed tier') at loop entry; absence of any escalation re-dispatch log line.

**Rollback:** `git revert <hash>` — harvest_mode defaults OFF so revert is behavior-neutral; the flag is purely additive.

**Test plan:** hermetic — a loop with harvest_mode=True and a stubbed device that returns a capability-fail signal must NOT re-dispatch at a higher escalation_hop (assert the driver stops the walk). PROOF NODE: with harvest_mode on, a capability failure yields a terminal 'stuck-at-wall' result and the escalation counter never increments — red (today's code escalates) → green via proof_emitter.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
